front: Exhibition: canceled by the Corcoran Gallery in Washington D.C. amid Congressional outcry over obscenity in photographs
Artist: Senator Jesse Helms called for ending NEA funding over this photographer's works
Exhibition: the Cincinnati Contemporary Arts Center was prosecuted for obscenity for displaying this photography exhibition
Photograph: Jim and Tom Sausalito depicts a hooded man urinating into another man's mouth
Exhibition: included a suggestive image of five-year-old Jesse McBride
Artist: divided 39 works into X, Y, and Z Portfolios
Work: 1986 photo collection criticized for exploitative fetishization of Black men, featuring nude African-American men including actor Derrick Cross
Artist: Glenn Ligon paired photographs from this artist's collection with 78 quotes describing their 'degradation of blackness'
Photograph: late self-portrait in a black turtleneck, grasping a cane topped with a skull, taken a year before this artist's 1989 death
Photograph: self-portrait showing the artist holding a rifle in front of a black five-pointed star, referencing Patty Hearst and the SLA symbol
Photograph: self-portrait in which the artist inserts a whip into his anus
Photograph: cover of Patti Smith's debut album Horses (1975), showing her androgynously with jacket over shoulder and black ribbon around neck
Photograph: a portrait of Patti Smith by this photographer imitates a self-portrait by Albrecht Dürer
Artist: Patti Smith described their relationship in her 2010 National Book Award-winning memoir Just Kids
Artist: Patti Smith supported this artist financially while he made altars from found objects
Artist: lived at the Chelsea Hotel with Patti Smith in 1970s New York
Photograph: shows Louise Bourgeois smiling, holding her phallic sculpture Fillette under her arm, wearing a black feathery coat
Work: 1983 book of photographs of bodybuilder Lisa Lyon, depicted nude, veiled, corseted, and holding a python
Artist: died in March 1989 of complications from AIDS
Artist: most expensive photograph by this artist was a platinum print portrait of Andy Warhol
Artist: works donated to the Getty Research Institute in Los Angeles after this photographer's death
Artist: shot close-ups of calla lilies and other flowers; made most money selling flower still lifes
Artist: described photographic approach as 'I see things like they were sculptures'
Artist: encouraged Tom of Finland to exhibit in mainstream art galleries
Artist: works juxtaposed with Michelangelo's David at 'Perfection in Form', the first modern art exhibition at the Accademia Gallery
Artist: Cindy Sherman was inspired by this photographer for her Sex series featuring pieced-together medical dummies
Artist: Don Herron photographed this artist and Keith Haring lying in their bathtubs as part of sixty-five black-and-white portraits of New York City residents
Artist: shot a portrait of poet Eileen Myles for the cover of her novel Chelsea Girls
